label: Firing notice (implicit sadness)
raw_text: He opened the envelope and read the letter twice. The company was restructuring. His position had been eliminated. He folded the letter carefully and placed it back in the envelope. He looked out the window at the parking lot where his car sat in the same spot it had occupied for eleven years.

label: Wedding day (implicit joy)
raw_text: She stood in front of the mirror and adjusted the veil one last time. Through the window she could hear the guests arriving, car doors closing, laughter carrying across the garden. Her mother appeared in the doorway and pressed a hand to her mouth. Neither of them spoke for a moment. Then her mother crossed the room and held her.

label: Dark alley (implicit fear)
raw_text: The streetlight at the end of the block had gone out again. She walked faster, keys threaded between her fingers, listening to footsteps that might have been an echo of her own. A car passed and its headlights swept the walls and then it was dark again. She didn't look back. She counted the steps to her building entrance.

label: Bureaucracy (implicit anger)
raw_text: He had been transferred to the fourth department in two hours. Each person asked him to fill out the same form. Each person told him the previous person should not have sent him here. The clock on the wall showed four-thirty. The office closed at five. He sat down in the plastic chair and stared at the form and breathed through his nose.

label: Neutral (no strong emotion)
raw_text: She checked the departure board and confirmed the train was on time. She found a seat on the platform and placed her bag on her lap. The station was moderately busy. She checked the board once more and waited.
